title: Who are the Nebraksa CornHuskers of the NFL
text:
Every football team I have ever cheered for sucked. In highschool we barely won any games. I have been a Husskers fan for life as well as a Bengals fan.


Code

title: About
text: I am Harrison Skiles, a broadcasting student and life long sports fan. As a sports fan I like breaking down the numbers ands seeing if I can find a different story in them.

title: Is Joe Burrow the next Andrew Luck?
text:
explain graphs here


Code
library(readr)
library(tidyverse)


в”Ђв”Ђ Attaching core tidyverse packages в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ tidyverse 2.0.0 в”Ђв”Ђ
вњ” dplyr     1.1.4     вњ” purrr     1.2.1
вњ” forcats   1.0.1     вњ” stringr   1.6.0
вњ” ggplot2   4.0.1     вњ” tibble    3.3.1
вњ” lubridate 1.9.4     вњ” tidyr     1.3.2
в”Ђв”Ђ Conflicts в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ tidyverse_conflicts() в”Ђв”Ђ
вњ– dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
вњ– dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
в„№ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors


Code
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)




Code
joeb <- read_csv("data/joeb.csv")


New names:
Rows: 6 Columns: 34
в”Ђв”Ђ Column specification
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ Delimiter: "," chr
(5): Team, Lg, Pos, QBrec, Awards dbl (29): Season, Age, G, GS, Cmp, Att, Cmp%,
Yds...12, TD, TD%, Int, Int%, ...
в„№ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. в„№
Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
вЂў `Yds` -> `Yds...12`
вЂў `Yds` -> `Yds...27`




Code
Aluck <- read_csv("data/andrewl.csv")


New names:
Rows: 6 Columns: 34
в”Ђв”Ђ Column specification
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ Delimiter: "," chr
(5): Team, Lg, Pos, QBrec, Awards dbl (29): Season, Age, G, GS, Cmp, Att, Cmp%,
Yds...12, TD, TD%, Int, Int%, ...
в„№ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. в„№
Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
вЂў `Yds` -> `Yds...12`
вЂў `Yds` -> `Yds...27`




Code
joebs <- joeb 
 Als <- Aluck
ggplot() +
    geom_line(data= joebs, aes (x= Season, y= Cmp), color="orange") +
    geom_line(data= Als, aes (x= Season, y= Cmp), color="navy") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 600))









Code
plot_annotation(
    title = "Is Joe Burrow the next Andrew Luck?",
    subtitle = "A season by season look to see how Burrow's early carrer compares to the trajectory of Andrew Luck.",
    caption = "Source: Sports Reference | By Harrison Skiles"
  )  & 
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8), 
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )


NULL


This graph shows how many passes Joe Burrow and Andrew Luck completed each season. It makes it easy to see how they compare over time, including who was more consistent and how they improved. Overall, it helps show if Burrow is following a similar path to Luck or doing something different.


Code
joetotal <- joebs |>
  summarise(
    totalcmp = sum(Cmp),
    totalatt = sum(Att),
    totaltd = sum(TD),
    season= Season
  ) |>
  mutate(
    passing_avg = totalcmp/totalatt
  )


Warning: Returning more (or less) than 1 row per `summarise()` group was deprecated in
dplyr 1.1.0.
в„№ Please use `reframe()` instead.
в„№ When switching from `summarise()` to `reframe()`, remember that `reframe()`
  always returns an ungrouped data frame and adjust accordingly.




Code
Atotal <- Als |>
  summarise(
    totalcmp = sum(Cmp),
    totalatt = sum(Att),
    totaltd = sum(TD),
    season = Season
  ) |>
  mutate(
    passing_avg = totalcmp/totalatt
  )


Warning: Returning more (or less) than 1 row per `summarise()` group was deprecated in
dplyr 1.1.0.
в„№ Please use `reframe()` instead.
в„№ When switching from `summarise()` to `reframe()`, remember that `reframe()`
  always returns an ungrouped data frame and adjust accordingly.




Code
Joe <- ggplot() + 
  geom_bar(data=joeb, aes(x=reorder(Season, Yds...12), weight=Yds...12)) +
  coord_flip() + 
  labs(
    x="", 
    y="Joe's Average Season passing yards"
    ) + 
  theme_minimal()

Andrew <- ggplot() + 
  geom_bar(data=Als, aes(x=reorder(Season, Yds...12), weight=Yds...12), color="navy") +
  coord_flip() +  
  labs(
    x="", 
    y="Andrew's Average Season Passing yards") + 
  theme_minimal()

Joe + Andrew +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Is Joe Burrow the next Andrew Luck?",
    subtitle = "A season by season look to see how Burrow's early carrer compares to the trajectory of Andrew Luck.",
    caption = "Source: Sports Reference | By Harrison Skiles"
  )  & 
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8), 
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )









This graph shows the average passing yards per season for Joe Burrow and Andrew Luck, giving a simple look at how many yards they threw for each year. It helps compare their production over time and shows how consistent each quarterback was throughout their seasons.


Code
ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    data= joeb,
    aes(
      y=Sk, yend=Sk,
      x= Season, xend= Season) ,
    color="white" ,
    linewidth = 1.5
    ) +
  geom_point(
    data= joeb,
    aes(
      y=Sk,
      x=Season,
      color="Joe Burrow"
    ), 
    size=2
  )+
  geom_point(
    data= Aluck,
    aes(
      y=Sk,
      x=Season,
      color="Andrew Luck"
    ),
    size=2
    )+
  scale_color_manual(name="Sacks Taken", values=c("navy", "orange")
  )









Code
plot_annotation(
    title = "Injuries have effected both of these quarterbacks",
    subtitle = "A look into how many sacks both have taken.",
    caption = "Source: Sports Reference | By Harrison Skiles"
  )  & 
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8), 
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )


NULL


This graph compares how often Joe Burrow and Andrew Luck were sacked throughout their careers, showing the pressure each quarterback faced behind their offensive lines. By looking at sacks taken season by season, it highlights how both players dealt with protection issues, durability challenges, and the impact constant pressure can have on performance and longevity.
